revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]][[ja:雨の宮古墳群]]
{{Short description|Kofun period tumili in Nakanoto, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Amenomiya Kofun Group | native_name = 雨の宮古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Amenomiya1-20 2010.jpg; File:Amenomiya1-20 2010.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Amenomiya Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Amenomiya Kofun Group | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|58|15|N|136|51|38|E|display=inline, title|qq=}}; {{Coord|36.975806|N|136.871533|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|qid=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto, Ishikawa|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = ''Kofun'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 79,045 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Amenomiya Kofun Group'''|雨の宮古墳群|Amenomiya kofun-gun|qq=}} is a group of middle[[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumuli]] located in the {{ill|Rokusei, Ishikawa|en|Rokusei, Ishikawa|qid=Q8193722|lt=Rokusei|1=Rokusei, Ishikawa}} neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|qid=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto, Ishikawa|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name = "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216339 |title= 雨の宮古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview==
The Amenomiya Kofun Group consists of 36 ancient tombs dating from the middleof the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century centered on Mount Bijo-san, a 188-meter hill in Nakanoto. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> Tomb No.1, located on the highest elevation is the largest "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}) tumulus in Ishikawa Prefecture, with an overall length of 64 meters. The posterior is 43.6 meters long by 8.5 meters high and the anterior is 31 meters long. It was made by partially cutting away a natural hill, and by using two different types of soil in alternate layers for strength and stability. It is a two-stage tumulus and was covered with ''[[fukiishi]].'' These ''fukiishi'' are in three sizes, with larger stones 40 to 60 centimeters used in the bottom tier, smaller stones 2 to 30 centimeters in the upper tier, and smallest stones (10 centimeters) as filler in-between the larger stones. The stones are in a variety of materials, some of which can only be found at considerable distances from the site. Two burial facilities were confirmed on the flat surface at the posterior top. The first is located approximately in the center, and the second burial facility is located to the west of it. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin that was 6.2 meters long and 80 centimeters wide with a rope hook on both the lid and the body. There is evidence that it was robbed in antiquity; however, some remaining {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], 165 metal artifacts, 34 jade beads, and fragments of lacquerware and pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/> These artifacts were collectively designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 2008.<ref name = "Bunka2">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/136267 |title= 石川県雨の宮一号墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> The second burial chamber is made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} stone slabs, but has not been opened. A portion of the tumulus was damaged by the {{ill|2007 Noto earthquake|en|2007 Noto earthquake|qid=Q986103|lt=2007 Noto earthquake|1=2007 Noto earthquake}}. Tomb No.2 is a round keyhole-shaped tumulus, {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, having one square end and one circular end, located to the northeast of Tomb No.1. It has an overall length of 65 meters, with a circular portion 42 meters in diameter, and the width of the rectangular port of 28 meters, narrowing in 25.5 meter where the two portions join. It also has two tier construction and uses ''fukiishi''. There is no evidence of tampering, and it is possible that the burial chamber is intact; it has not been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The other tumuli in the cluster are mostly smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}). Per the Ishikawa Archaeological Society, the tumuli are as follows: {| class="wikitable" ! Designation !! type !! Diameter !! Height (m) !! Comments |- | 1号墳|| co-joined rectangle || || || See text above |- | 2号墳|| keyhole-shaped || || || See text above |- | 3号墳|| Circular || 30 || 4.5 || known as "Atagozuka" |- | 4号墳|| Circular || 20 || 2.5 || evidence of grave robbery |- | 5号墳|| Circular || 13 || 2 || |- | 6号墳|| Circular || 12.5 || 1.5～2 || |- | 7号墳|| Circular || 8 || 2 || |- | 8号墳|| Circular || 12 || 1.5 || |- | 9号墳|| Circular || 10 || 1.5 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 10号墳|| Circular || 14 || 1.8 || |- | 11号墳|| Circular || 12 || 1.5 || |- | 12号墳|| Circular || 8 || 1.5 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 13号墳|| Circular || 17 || 2.8 || |- | 14号墳|| Circular || 10 || 1.0 || |- | 15号墳|| Circular || 6 || 0.8 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 16号墳|| Circular || 8 || 1.0 || bisected by a road |- | 17号墳|| Circular || 16 || 1.5 || stone sarcophagus found |- | 18号墳|| Circular || unknown || unknown || only the edges remain |- | 19号墳|| Circular || unknown || unknown || only the edges remain |- | 20号墳|| Circular || 22 || 2.2 || low mound, like a cushion |- | 21号墳|| Circular || 12 || 1.5 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 22号墳|| Circular || 13 || 1.8 || Modified on one side |- | 23号墳|| Circular || 11 || 2.0 || Modified on one side |- | 24号墳|| Circular || 18 || 2.5 || Modified on one side |- | 25号墳|| Circular || unknown || unknown || only the edges remain |- | 26号墳|| Circular || 20 || 2.5～3 || mostly destroyed |- | 27号墳|| Circular || 15 || 1 || possibly square-shaped |- | 28号墳|| Circular || 12 || 1.2 || may be a natural hill |- | 29号墳|| Circular || 12 || 1.2 || may be a natural hill |- | 30号墳|| Circular || 10 || 1.2 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 31号墳|| Circular || 12 || 2 || irregular shape; may be a natural hill |- | 32号墳|| Oval || 9.0×14.0 || 1.3～1.0 || |- | 33号墳|| Circular || 7.0 || 0.5～0.0 || |- | 34号墳|| Circular || 9.0 || 1.0～0.0 || |- | 35号墳|| Circular || 21 || 2.0～1.0 || damaged by road on the west |- | 36号墳|| Circular || 7.0 || 0.5 || damaged by road on the west |- | 37号墳|| Circular || 10.0 || 0.5 || |- | 38号墳|| Square || 8.5×10.0 || 0.5 || |- | 39号墳|| Circular || 13.0 || 2.5～1.0 || damaged on the south side |- | R35号墳|| Square (?) || 10～5 || 0.5 || Debris from No.1; possibly not a tomb |- | R36号墳|| Square || 11 || 0.5 || beads found; possibly a ritual site and not a tomb |} The site is preserved as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with the {{nihono|'''Ame-no-miya Noto Royal Tomb Museum'''|雨の宮能登王墓の館|Ame no miya Noto ō bo no yakata|qq=}}. It is located about 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Notobe Station|en|Notobe Station|qid=Q7063127|lt=Notobe Station|1=Notobe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|qid=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line|1=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Gallery== <gallery> Noto-oubo-no-yakata.jpg|Ame-no-miya Noto Royal Tomb Museum Amenomiya 17g 2010.jpg|Amenomiya Kofun No.17 Amenomiya guide collapse.jpg|Damage to Amenomiya Kofun No.1 </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|qid=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Amenomiya Kofun Group|qq=}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-5.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/tourist_navi/kanko_spot/3434.html Nakanoto town home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} Amenomiya Kofun Cluster
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
